summary: All four BlarAI trust-root TPM keys are RESIDENT, FUNCTIONAL, and NON-EXPORTABLE on the real chip, each proven directly per key. Upgrades the #612 capstone S-K 'Is It Real?' trust-root claim (item 1) and the SECURITY_ROADMAP 'TPM key state' table from disk-inferred to chip-verified.
scope_note: Scopes the trust-root VERIFICATION criterion only. It does NOT open the #598 air-gap GO/NO-GO gate, which still gates on the #612 capstone phase (roadmap S5.13) + the LA sign-off (S5.12), the #106 signed-manifest-runtime remainder, the DORMANT egress machinery (post-#556), and #607.

tpm.also_present: Microsoft Pluton security processor (PCI\VEN_8086&DEV_A862) is present on this unit but is NOT serving as the TPM 2.0 role.

environment.os: Windows 11 Pro (10.0.26200); note: platform.platform() reports 'Windows-10-10.0.26200-SP0' (a known platform string quirk on Windows 11)
environment.shell_context: elevated (administrator) — the same context the provisioning/boot path uses. is_available() returns False off-box or from an unprovisioned context.

methodology.read_only_guarantee: The probe calls ONLY read-only primitives: is_available, key_exists, sign, verify, export_public_key (signers) and TpmSealer(auto_provision=False).seal/.unseal (sealer). It calls NO ensure_key, NO delete_key, NO NCryptCreatePersistedKey, NO provisioning script. A refused private-key export reads-and-fails and mutates nothing.

per_key_checks.functional_roundtrip: signers: sign(fixed non-secret test message) -> verify() == True (on-chip ECDSA P-256); sealer: TpmSealer(auto_provision=False).seal(os.urandom(32)) -> unseal() == that throwaway 32-byte blob (on-chip RSA-2048 OAEP-SHA256). The real DEK and the keystore file are never used.
per_key_checks.private_export_refused: a single direct per-key NCryptExportKey for the PRIVATE blob (ECCPRIVATEBLOB for the ECDSA keys, RSAFULLPRIVATEBLOB for the RSA key) returns a non-SUCCESS status (export REFUSED by the provider). This is the exact CNG call vetted in test_tpm_signer.py:98 / test_tpm_sealer.py:206, pointed at the production key handles — a true per-production-key proof, not verified-by-equivalence.

not_measured: Per-operation TPM signing/sealing latency was not measured in this run (the GOV-10 hot-path budget and timings are tracked separately in PERFORMANCE_LOG.md / docs/performance/).
PCR-binding policy (NCRYPT_PCP_* / measured-boot binding) was not exercised — these keys are not PCR-bound; that is a future attestation increment (see TPM_CAPABILITY_FINDINGS.md S2/S3).
Reachability of the four PRODUCTION keys from a NON-elevated shell was not established by this run, and is not required: the boot/provisioning path runs in the elevated context used here.
methodology.records_only: booleans, key names, verdicts, and test results ONLY — no private key material, no raw signatures, no DEK, and no throwaway test blob are recorded in this artifact.

keys.role: Policy Agent DecisionArtifact JWT minting (5 s TTL)
tamper-evident audit stream (hash-chained record signing; separation of duties from the JWT key)
FUT-04 weight-integrity manifest signing (ADR-018; require_signed_manifest=true since Sprint 18)
at-rest Data Encryption Key (DEK) envelope, RSA-2048 OAEP-SHA256 (ADR-025)
keys.note: The sealer exposes no public-export API, so public_export_ok is N/A (null).

hardware_tests.command: pytest shared/tests/test_tpm_signer.py shared/tests/test_tpm_sealer.py shared/tests/test_tpm_record_signer.py shared/tests/test_verify_trust_root.py -m slow
includes: test_tpm_record_signer.py::TestTpmRecordSignerHardware (production audit key real sign/verify round-trip, read-only: ensure_key no-ops on the pre-provisioned key, no delete) — 4
test_verify_trust_root.py::TestVerifyTrustRootHardware (new — probe logic validated on EPHEMERAL keys, never the four production keys) — 3

ceremony_preflight.result: READY for production boot — all 9 checks OK

downstream_actions.capstone_deck_edit_handed_to_deck_builde: deck_outline.json: (1) line ~972 S-K 'Is It Real?' — move the 'four TPM keys physically chip-resident is INFERRED' clause from NOT-verified to VERIFIED-LIVE (keep the other three NOT-verified items); (2) line ~104 trust-root slide — regrade item 1 from 'TESTED, not VERIFIED-LIVE' to 'VERIFIED-LIVE'; (3) line ~630 transformation slide — drop the 'chip residency is disk-inferred' caveat. Cite this artifact. Re-render capstone_presentation.html / EXECUTIVE_SUMMARY.md / coverage.md from the outline. (verify_trust_root does NOT edit the deck — deck-builder scope.)
downstream_actions.stale_doc_finding: SECURITY_ROADMAP_air_gap_removal.md:263 'TPM key state' table (dated 2026-06-07 snapshot) marks BlarAI-Manifest-Signing 'NOT PROVISIONED' and 'preflight does not yet probe for it' — both now false. Recommended one-line update: mark all four keys VERIFIED-LIVE (2026-06-09) citing this artifact. Surfaced for the roadmap owner (out of this session's write-scope).

references.roadmap: docs/security/SECURITY_ROADMAP_air_gap_removal.md (S5.1 production-posture verification umbrella; 'TPM key state' section)
references.capstone_ticket: #612 (upgrades the S-K 'Is It Real?' trust-root claim, item 1)
references.gate_ticket: #598 (air-gap GO/NO-GO — NOT opened by this verification)
references.adrs: ADR-018 (TPM trust-root primitive), ADR-021 (TPM-sealed PA JWT key), ADR-025 (at-rest DEK envelope)
